name: Alder Buckthorn
shop_description:
"Unlike other buckthorns, the Alder Buckthorn does not have thorns."

 

Consuming this plant permanently increases Intelligence by +10.

name: Bear Clover
shop_description:
"A tasty, and occasionally lucky, snack!"

Eating Bear Clover ensures Herbs will be dropped during the next two rolls.

name: Bunchgrass
shop_description:
"Many species have long roots that may reach 2 meters (6.6 ft) or more into the soil, which can aid slope stabilization, erosion control, and soil porosity for precipitation absorption."

Consuming this plant removes one Benefit or Detriment from an NPC.

name: California Rock Parsnip
shop_description:
"The Yuki chewed this plant while hunting to prevent deer from detecting human scents, while the Chumash called it chuchupaste and used it to cure headaches and stomach pain."

This plant halves the amount of points required to claim a horse.

name: Chaparral Currant
shop_description:
"Chaparral Currant thrives under oaks in bright, dry conditions."

This plant provides a permanent +2 boost to all stats!

name: Creeping Sage
shop_description:
"There are several varieties of this plant, all based on the different leaf types and flower colors."

Consuming this plant will provide a permanent +2 boost to one stat of your choosing!

name: Dogwood
shop_description:
"The dogwood tree contains both male and female sexual reproductive organs."

Eating Dogwood ensures a Statue will be dropped during in the next roll.

name: Greasewood
shop_description:
"Prefers limestone substrates."

This plant guarantees a pregnancy for one mare.

name: Joshua Leaves
shop_description:
"Young Joshua Leaves are ideal food for a variety of animals."

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Charisma by +10. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: Lilac
shop_description:
"A sweet-smelling flower that only blooms in late spring or early summer."

This plant guarantees the sex of a foal to be female.

name: Owl Clover
shop_description:
"Provides a splash of color and is ideal for nectoring insects."

Calms foals during events, preventing their separation from the herd.

name: Ragwort
shop_description:
"Flowers from July to October."

Calms one mare or stallion during events, preventing their separation from the herd.

name: Scented Shooting Star
shop_description:
"The Scented Shooting Star points downward to the ground, gradually pointing skyward with age."

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Strength by +10. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: Serviceberry
shop_description:
"Does well in acidic and well-drained soils."

 

Eating Serviceberry ensures Stones will be dropped during the next two rolls.

name: Sugar Pine Needles
shop_description:
"The Sugar Pine grows slowly while young and at a much more rapid pace as it ages."

Sugar Pine Needles are ideal for small cuts and bruises, healing a horse for +2% of its health.

name: Tree Anemone
shop_description:
"Not an actual cnidarian."

 

This plant will heal a horse for 10% of its health pool.

name: Ash Leaves
shop_description:
"Ash trees are integral to healthy soils."

Ash Leaves are ideal for fighting infection; the use of this item will remove all damage over time (DOT) effects.

name: Bitter Gooseberry
shop_description:
"Some types of gooseberry turn red, white, yellow, green or pink when it’s gooseberry harvest time, but the best way to tell if they are ripe is to squeeze them gently."

Consuming this plant permanently increases Strength by +10.

name: California Buckeye
shop_description:
"Did you know? Buckeye are not considered true nuts."

 

Eating this plant will heal your horse to full health unless your horse is in critical (0 HP or less) health.

name: Coffeeberry
shop_description:
"Although the plant itself looks much like a coffee plant, its berries, which are succulent, do not."

Consuming this plant permanently increases Stamina by +10.

name: Deer Fern
shop_description:
"A deep shade-loving fern with two distinctly different fronds."

Deer Fern causes your herd to move swiftly, allowing them to fast-travel anywhere on the map!

name: Gray Mule's Ears
shop_description:
"A flowering species of plant in the sunflower family."

Gray Mule's Ears are a favorite of burros, donkeys, and mules - even the mere presence of the plant is enough to lure one to join your herd.

name: Harlequin Lupine
shop_description:
"The fruit of this flower is a legume pod around 2 centimeters long containing usually 5 seeds."

This plant attracts one mare of your choice and auto-claims them. May be designed.

name: Juniper Berries
shop_description:
"Not a true berry, instead a cone."

 

Juniper Berries allow the redesign of one horse, excluding history.

name: Mahogany Bark
shop_description:
"Characterized by the rich, brown-red color and striped appearance."

This bark attracts one stallion to your herd and auto-claims them. May be designed.

name: Monkeyflower
shop_description:
Not available in Welcome Packages.

"The blossoms resemble a monkey's face!"

 

Monkeyflower cures all ailments, including serious injury, and restores a horse to full health. Usable during death saving throws.

name: Phlox
shop_description:
"A low-growing plant, excellent for ground cover."

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Intellect by +10. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: Purple Mouse Ear
shop_description:
"The success of some plant species comes from the simple fact that they can grow where other plants can't. Such is the case for the Purple Mouse Ear."

 

Consuming this plant adds one Benefit or Detriment to an NPC. May be random or from the player's Vault.

name: Redbud
shop_description:
"Blooms in April."

Consuming Redbud permanently increases Charisma by +10.

name: Scrub Oak Leaf
shop_description: Scrub Oak Leaves will cause a pregnancy to fail.

name: Spiny Hopsage
shop_description:
"This plant flowers and fruits in May through June."

Consuming this plant permanently increases Wisdom by +10.

name: Teal Lovegrass
shop_description:
"Teal Lovegrass is found in moist areas near water in substrates of sand or mud."

A favorite of all equines, Teal Lovegrass will increase a herd's unity score by +10 points.

name: Wild Carrot
shop_description:
"Like the cultivated carrot, the root is edible while young, but it quickly becomes too woody to consume."

A favorite of all equines, the Wild Carrot will increase a herd's unity score by +5 points.

name: Yosemite Onion
shop_description:
"The Yosemite Onion is listed by the State of California as a Rare plant."

Increases a horse's chance of finding items by 50% for five consecutive submissions.

name: Azalea
shop_description:
"Nothing is more beautiful than an Azalea shrub in bloom!"

Consuming an Azalea will allow a mare to given birth out of season.

name: Buckwheat
shop_description:
"Buckwheat is rich in fiber."

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Dexterity by +10. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: California Flannelbush
shop_description:
"This plant is highly adapted to withstand natural forest fires."

Eating some Flannelbush will protect your herd from fire-related events.

name: Cedar Bough
shop_description:
"Cedar is aromatic and a natural insect repellent."

Eating a Cedar Bough ensures a Feather will be dropped in the next roll.

name: Common Sagebrush
shop_description:
"A perennial plant with stiff, straight stems."

Common Sagebrush attracts one foal to your herd and auto-claims them. May be designed.

name: Desert Parsley
shop_description:
"In addition to being particularly tasty, Desert Parsley might help to eliminate different types of bacteria, fungi, or viruses that can cause infections."

This herb will waive all secret number requirements while claiming a new herd member.

name: Golden Currant
shop_description:
"Unlike some other species of currants, Ribes aureum is in the remarkably drought-tolerant group of Ribes."

Consuming this plant permanently increases Dexterity by +10.

name: Iris
shop_description:
Not available in Welcome Packages.

"Popular in gardens for their showy flowers."

Allows the import of one horse with a breed other than Mustang. Does not include Draft/Cold-blooded breeds.

name: Knobcone Pinecone
shop_description:
"Knobcone are the most widely distributed of the West Coast closed-cone pine species."

Consuming this Pinecone permanently increases Constitution by +10.

name: Manzanita
shop_description:
"The berries are edible - and delicious!"

This flower ensures that a mare will have a healthy pregnancy.

name: Oak Leaf
shop_description:
"Most oak leaves can be identified by their somewhat lobed leaves."

These leaves are able to heal most minor injuries, healing your horse for 5% of its health.

name: Ponderosa Pine Needles
shop_description:
"Ponderosa Pine Needles can be up to seven inches long!"

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Health by +10%. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: Purple Sage
shop_description:
"Purple Sage can be made into teas for medicinal purposes."

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Constitution by +10. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: Red Sierra Onion
shop_description:
"This plant tends to grow in granitic sands, at elevations 2600-11500 feet."

This plant is super healthy! It will protect one newborn foal from all illnesses and injuries.

name: Sierra Monardella
shop_description:
"Monardella is a nectar plant for many Lepidoptera, including the endangered Myrtle's silverspot."

Looking to respec? This plant will reset a horse's stats.

name: Spiny Redberry
shop_description:
"When eaten in large quantities, the berry is reported to impart a red tint to the entire body of the consumer."

Munching down on this plant gives your character a temporary boost to Wisdom by +10. Lasts for the next 3 submissions.

name: Toyon
shop_description:
"The berries of these trees, unlike some other holly trees, are edible."

This plant guarantees the sex of a foal to be male.

name: Willow Sprig
shop_description:
"Willows are susceptible to mildew, fungi, and root rot."

Willow Sprigs negate most poisons and toxins.
